items.title: Copper price slides as monster storm bears down on Chile and Iran flare-up rattles metals
items.summary: A potential Category 5 atmospheric river is battering the world’s biggest copper producer just as US strikes near Iran’s export terminal rattle metals markets.

items.title: Canada’s first diamond mine to shut down
items.summary: Ekati in the Northwest Territories will shut down following a Supreme Court of British Columbia ruling placed it into receivership this week.

items.title: Acid test: IEA warns copper supply outlook has “worsened considerably”
items.summary: Over a seventh of the world's copper mine output is now hostage to a sulphuric acid market thrown into chaos by the Middle East conflict and Beijing's export ban.

items.title: Hitachi Machinery, Pronto sign mine automation solutions MoU
items.summary: The companies said they aim to deliver automation solutions that provide customers with a broader range of options.

items.title: Copper price: BMI hikes forecasts – structural deficits to bring $17,000 next decade
items.summary: BMI's upgraded 2026 outlook still sits below consensus but from 2028 the Fitch unit out-bulls Wall Street, with deficits widening every year to nearly 1.5 million tonnes seen driv…

items.title: Central Nevada Gold looks to revive Mule Canyon mine
items.summary: It aims to restart the mine after acquiring it from Newmont, advancing studies, permits and a new resource.

items.title: BHP’s first port strike in 26 years rattles iron ore supply
items.summary: Union action at Port Hedland raises fresh concerns over iron ore exports as wage negotiations remain deadlocked.

items.title: Op-Ed: Mining’s hidden margin leak isn’t labour, it’s reliability
items.summary: The key difference between efficient and inefficient mines often lies in reliability, not in labour.

items.title: BHP copper output slips 5% in Q4, flags further decline in Chile
items.summary: Stronger prices cushioned the volume drop as BHP advanced Escondida, Cerro Colorado and its Argentina and Arizona copper projects to counter grade decline.

items.title: Glomar Minerals launches new research expedition in Pacific’s Clarion Clipperton Zone
items.summary: The new 75-day research mission will be the first to gather geological and biological samples in the company's second license area.

items.title: Cameco restarts Cigar Lake uranium mine
items.summary: On July 1, Cameco temporarily suspended operations, citing disruptions at the McClean Lake mill.

items.title: Greenland Mines lifts contained PGM by 31% at Skaergaard
items.summary: Skaergaard project boost positiosthe Greenland PGM deposit among top undeveloped assets.

items.title: Mining companies are on their own in the Sahel, says risk analyst
items.summary: Critical Risk Team's George McLeod says Russian setbacks, jihadist insurgencies and rising resource nationalism are reshaping the risk map for miners across West Africa and beyond.

items.title: May 2026 crude steel production
items.summary: World crude steel production for the 70 countries reporting to worldsteel was 157.9 million tonnes (Mt) in May 2026, a 0.3% decrease compared to May 2025.

items.title: World Steel in Figures 2026 now available
items.summary: World Steel in Figures provides a snapshot of of today’s steel industry, including top steel producers, production by country and processes, demand, trade, and much more.

items.title: April 2026 crude steel production
items.summary: World crude steel production for the 69 countries reporting to worldsteel was 153.4 million tonnes (Mt) in April 2026, a 1.9% decrease compared to April 2025.

items.title: worldsteel releases 2026 LCI database
items.summary: worldsteel is today releasing its global 2026 Life Cycle Inventory (LCI) database, based on 2024 data. The data captures the full “cradle‑to‑gate” picture of steel production.

items.title: March 2026 crude steel production
items.summary: World crude steel production for the 69 countries reporting to worldsteel was 159.9 million tonnes (Mt) in March 2026, a 4.2% decrease compared to March 2025.

items.title: worldsteel Short Range Outlook April 2026
items.summary: worldsteel today released its Short Range Outlook (SRO) steel demand forecast for 2026 and 2027. worldsteel forecasts that global demand will grow by 0.3% in 2026 to reach 1,724 M…

items.title: worldsteel announces the 2026 Steel Sustainability Champions
items.summary: worldsteel has recognised 14 companies as Steel Sustainability Champions for their work in 2025. Find out which companies are the 2026 Steel Sustainability Champions.

items.title: steelChallenge-20 World Champions announcement
items.summary: worldsteel today announced the new World Champions of the 20th steelChallenge. There are two winners, one in the industry category, one in the student category. Find out who they…

items.title: worldsteel welcomes new members and new Director General
items.summary: The Board of Members of worldsteel has today welcomed seven new regular members and one new affiliated member. We also announced that Dr. Henrik ADAM will lead the association as…

items.title: February 2026 crude steel production
items.summary: World crude steel production for the 69 countries reporting to worldsteel was 141.8 million tonnes (Mt) in February 2026, a 2.2% decrease compared to February 2025.

items.title: January 2026 crude steel production
items.summary: World crude steel production for the 69 countries reporting to worldsteel was 147.3 million tonnes (Mt) in January 2026, a 6.5% decrease compared to January 2025.

items.title: December 2025 crude steel production and 2025 global crude steel production totals
items.summary: World crude steel production for the 70 countries reporting to the worldsteel was 139.6 million tonnes (Mt) in December 2025, a 3.7% decrease compared to December 2024.

items.title: worldsteel releases indirect trade in steel data 2014 – 2024
